question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 91.00% on 2025-09-24 for the Metaculus question "Will Zohran Mamdani be elected Mayor of New York City in 2025?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the CP for "Will Zohran Mamdani be elected Mayor of NYC?" will exceed 91% on Sept 24. The underlying CP has been stable at 0.90 for 8 consecutive data points since Sept 7, trending down from a peak of 0.93 in early September. The historical transition rate from 0.90 to >0.91 is only 7.1% (1/14 observations). A previous meta-question with a lower threshold (>90%) settled at ~40% CP and resolved NO. With 133 forecasters, the CP is moderately sticky, and no obvious catalyst exists before Sept 24 (general election isn't until Nov 4). I forecast ~25% probability.

factors.description: CP has been stable at 0.90 for 8 consecutive data points since Sept 7, showing no movement toward the 91% threshold
Historical transition rate from CP=0.90 to CP>0.91 is only 7.1% (1 out of 14 transitions)
Clear downward trend from 0.93 peak (early Sept) to current 0.90 - forecasters actively moved CP away from the threshold
133 forecasters makes the CP moderately stable and hard to shift with individual forecasts
Previous meta-question (CP>90% on Aug 29) settled at ~40% and resolved NO, suggesting forecasters are skeptical of threshold-crossing; our threshold is even higher
CP was above 91% as recently as Sept 6 (0.93), showing the gap is small and oscillation is possible
9 days remain before resolution, providing time for potential CP movement from news or new forecasters

reasoning:

Let me get the CP history and question details.Now let me search for the latest news on this race and check the previous similar meta-question.Let me do a bit more analysis on the CP trajectory and search for recent election news.Now let me write my meta-reflection and produce the final forecast.**Forecast: 0.25 (25%)**

Key reasoning:

- **Current CP is 0.90**, one percentage point below the 91% threshold, and has been stuck there for 8 consecutive data points since Sept 7
- **Downward trend**: CP peaked at 0.93 in early September and was actively moved down by forecasters
- **Low transition rate**: Historically, when CP is at 0.90, it only moves above 0.91 about 7% of the time in the next observation
- **Previous meta-question**: A similar question asking about CP >90% on Aug 29 had forecasters at ~40% and resolved NO (underlying CP was exactly 0.90). Our threshold is *higher* (91% vs 90%)
- **Limited catalysts**: The NYC general election isn't until Nov 4, so no imminent event is likely to sharply shift forecaster confidence
- **Counterpoint**: The gap is small and CP has oscillated above 91% recently, so a bounce back isn't impossible — just not the base case
